source: Telegram UX review 2026-06-21 â€” legacy parse_mode='Markdown' silently drops replies containing stray * _ [ chars; media handlers already band-aided to plain text (T-138).
title: Robust Telegram formatting: HTML (or MarkdownV2) with proper escaping
current_state: _get_bot() sets parse_mode='Markdown' (the legacy mode). Any reply with unbalanced * _ [ ` raises a parse error and the message is silently dropped. T-138 worked around this for vision/STT output by forcing parse_mode=None (_send_plain_chunks), but ordinary text replies (_reply_chunks) still use fragile Markdown and can vanish.
target_state: A single _format_for_telegram(text) path renders reliably for ALL replies. Recommended: switch to HTML parse mode (only &, <, > need escaping â€” far more robust than MarkdownV2's 18 special chars), converting Pi's common markdown (bold, italics, inline code, code blocks, links) to safe HTML and escaping the rest. Replies never silently drop; the _send_plain_chunks band-aid can be retired (or kept as a final fallback).
migration_plan: 1. Add _format_for_telegram(text)->str: escape &,<,> then convert a safe subset of markdown (**bold**, _italic_, `code`, ```blocks```, [text](url)) to Telegram HTML tags. Leave everything else as escaped plain text.
2. Switch _get_bot() to parse_mode='HTML' (or pass per-message). Route _reply_chunks through _format_for_telegram.
3. Make send_message() apply the same formatting so the telegram_send TOOL benefits too.
4. Chunking must not split inside an HTML tag â€” chunk on safe boundaries (or close/reopen tags across chunk edges).
5. Keep a final fallback: if a formatted send still raises, retry once with parse_mode=None so a reply is NEVER lost.

6. Tests: new testing/test_telegram_formatting.py â€” text with stray * _ [ < & survives; bold/code/link render to correct HTML; chunk boundary never splits a tag; fallback path triggers on malformed input.
risk_notes: Self-contained formatting layer. HTML chosen over MarkdownV2 for a smaller escape surface (3 chars vs 18). Verify the existing media/voice handlers still read correctly once routed through the unified formatter; keep parse_mode=None fallback so nothing is ever silently dropped.
